title: Produce deep prior-art research artifact
description: Research prior art for CLI table parsing, Nushell converters/plugins, descriptor-driven parsers, terminal table extraction, and safe extension architectures. Produce a source-grounded artifact with findings, tradeoffs, links, and implications for Golden Magic architecture.
close_reason: Added docs/PRIOR-ART.md with source-grounded research and architecture implications.

title: Design safe runtime extension architecture
description: Define Golden Magic's non-footgun extension architecture before any arbitrary Rust runtime plugin loading. Evaluate native loading risks, subprocess descriptors, WASM boundaries, portability, trust model, versioning, and sandbox behavior. Produce design docs and implementation criteria before code.
close_reason: Added docs/EXTENSIONS.md defining descriptor, subprocess, WASM/WASI, and forbidden native-loading boundaries. Native runtime loading remains unimplemented by design.

title: Implement grammar engine
description: Evaluate parser-backend options before implementing a grammar layer. Treat tree-sitter as the first serious candidate for structured, language-like, or nested CLI output where simple table heuristics are insufficient. Do not assume a custom grammar DSL is required. Deliverables must include source-grounded prior-art notes, a decision record comparing tree-sitter/custom grammar/nom-style parser combinators/current heuristics, a narrow prototype if the decision supports it, stable rule ids, fixtures, property tests, diagnostics, and descriptor integration. If tree-sitter is rejected, document why with evidence.
notes: Implemented dependency-free sections backend as first real non-table backend: ParseOptions::backend("sections") parses repeated section: <name> blocks plus key: value fields, emits backend.sections/backend.sections.parsed/backend.sections.malformed trace events, has unit/property/CLI/descriptor fixture coverage, and appears in --list-backends. Remaining work: tree-sitter support or evidence-backed rejection with measured dependency/build assessment.
close_reason: Implemented parser-backend/grammar-like parsing slice: backend selection in core, descriptor backend validation/listing, heuristic and sections backends, stable trace ids, sectioned fixture corpus, unit/property/CLI/descriptor tests, malformed-input diagnostics, and documented tree-sitter deferral with evidence and future-entry criteria.

title: Build known-tool descriptor corpus
description: Create a meaningful known-tool descriptor corpus with fixtures. Each vendored descriptor needs isolated fixture coverage, negative input, expected rows, and full-registry conflict coverage. Start with a representative set of real CLI outputs rather than only generic delimiter examples.
close_reason: Added a representative known-tool descriptor corpus with fixture coverage under the current descriptor schema. Grammar-heavy real outputs remain covered by golden-magic-2mf.

title: Implement descriptor-driven Nix fixture manifest harness
description: Replace the hardcoded optional Nix smoke with a manifest-driven harness for descriptor fixtures. The harness should read fixture metadata, enter nix shell with declared packages, run the declared command, parse through golden-magic with the fixture descriptor, compare expected rows, skip unless GOLDEN_MAGIC_RUN_NIX_FIXTURES=1, and document the format.
notes: Implemented stricter opt-in behavior for the descriptor-driven Nix fixture harness: default cargo test still skips when GOLDEN_MAGIC_RUN_NIX_FIXTURES is absent, but GOLDEN_MAGIC_RUN_NIX_FIXTURES=1 now requires a working nix binary and fails loudly if nix is missing instead of reporting a false pass. Verified cargo fmt --check, cargo clippy -- -D warnings, cargo test, cargo test --features nu-plugin, cargo test --test performance_gate -- --nocapture, and cargo test --test nix_fixture -- --nocapture. Live Nix fixture execution remains unproven on this machine because nix/nix-shell/determinate-nix are not on PATH; GOLDEN_MAGIC_RUN_NIX_FIXTURES=1 cargo test --test nix_fixture -- --nocapture fails with 'requires nix on PATH'. Do not close until run successfully in a Nix-enabled environment or an explicit separate live-verification bead supersedes that requirement.

title: Add native Nu plugin descriptor and config loading
description: Native nu_plugin_golden_magic should support descriptor/config loading parity with the CLI path: explicit descriptor dirs, XDG/default config discovery, no-default opt-out, descriptor rule validation, selected descriptor trace, and integration coverage.
close_reason: Implemented native Nu plugin descriptor/config loading parity with integration coverage.

title: Create and push public GitHub repo
description: Create the matching public GitHub repository for Golden Magic and push the local git history after Ramiro explicitly approves the public side effect.
notes: Blocked pending explicit approval from Ramiro to create a public GitHub repository and push local history.
close_reason: Created public GitHub repository and pushed local history to origin/master.

title: Implement generic Golden Magic core
description: Rust parser core, CLI adapter, Nushell wrapper, descriptor registry/XDG config, tests, docs, and benchmark harness for the initial generic Golden Magic slice.
close_reason: Implemented initial generic core slice with CLI, Nu wrapper, descriptors, tests, docs, and benchmarks.

title: Investigate Criterion benchmark regression
description: Completion audit found that cargo bench --bench parser -- --sample-size 10 runs successfully but reports local Criterion baseline regressions while the hard performance gate passes. Investigate whether this is noise, stale baseline drift, or a real parser regression; update baselines only with an explicit tradeoff note.
close_reason: Investigated Criterion output: hard perf gate passes, Criterion exits 0, medium TSV shows small regression against untracked local target/criterion baseline while large/header improved. Documented current results and baseline policy in docs/PERFORMANCE.md without updating baselines.

title: Create extension-author SDK
description: Create documentation, schemas, validation tooling, examples, and test harness support for descriptor or future extension authors. Must cover descriptor authoring, fixture layout, rule ids, diagnostics, compatibility/versioning, and publishing guidance.
close_reason: Descriptor-pack SDK implemented with validation CLI, schema, docs, examples, fixture guidance, and CLI tests.

title: Build descriptor fixture harness
description: Create isolated descriptor fixture tests covering descriptor selection, negative matches, expected parsed output, and full-registry conflict behavior.
close_reason: Implemented descriptor fixture harness with isolated positive/negative fixtures, expected row assertions, and full-registry duplicate-id coverage.

title: Implement native Nushell plugin adapter
description: Replace or complement the CLI-backed nu/golden-magic.nu wrapper with a native Nushell plugin command shaped like from golden-magic, backed by the existing parser core.
close_reason: Added native nu_plugin_golden_magic binary exporting from golden-magic and integration-tested isolated Nu plugin registration/use.

title: Design debug instrumentation protocol
description: Security-review and document any harness-only debug channel before implementation; avoid hidden stdout/stderr interference.
close_reason: Documented debug instrumentation threat model, explicit trace-first policy, and strict gates for any future harness-only side channel.

title: Promote performance baselines into hard gates
description: Turn docs/PERFORMANCE.md baselines into explicit budgets and CI/check commands with acceptable tradeoff documentation.
close_reason: Added cargo-test performance regression gates and documented conservative budgets alongside Criterion baselines.

title: Add Nix-backed CLI fixture isolation
description: Design and implement optional Nix-based setup/teardown for tests that need real third-party CLIs without installing system-wide dependencies.
close_reason: Added optional Nix-backed integration fixture, opt-in execution gate, and documentation for non-system CLI isolation.
